user_message: Quick one -- pull the context on Consolidated Messenger. Not urgent but would be good to have by EOD.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Singh <sarah.singh@consolidated.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-20 15:12 EST
Subject: URGENT: whitespace analysis Issue at Consolidated Messenger

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about whitespace analysis. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Sarah Singh <sarah.singh@consolidated.com>
Date: 2024-01-15 12:28 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Consolidated Messenger whitespace analysis

Our team encountered some challenges with whitespace analysis. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: whitespace analysis Discussion - Consolidated Messenger
Date: 2024-02-11
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), David (Consolidated Messenger)

[00:01:15] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is whitespace analysis.

[00:06:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:10:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:12:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the whitespace analysis front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:16:00] David (Consolidated Messenger): Let me add some context -- we've been working on stakeholder alignment and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:19:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:24:00] David (Consolidated Messenger): Let me add some context -- we've been working on stakeholder alignment and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:29:00] David (Consolidated Messenger): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:33:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Pre-Meeting Brief template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct whitespace analysis decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
